attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 200-300m, often from too far out causing premature fading. Rare bridging capability after crashes (2017) but not a tactical attacker.
team_tactics: Quick-Step/UAE built full leadout trains 2017-2018 with Richeze as final pilot; post-2019 increasingly reliant on positioning behind rival trains rather than own team control, indicating diminished leadout resources or confidence.
terrain_preference: Excels on technical, downhill finishes using track background for superior bike handling. Exposed on any uphill gradient or climbs beyond 10km; struggles in sustained headwinds when launching early.
vulnerability: Chronic sprint timing judgment issues—launches too early and fades in final 50m (observed 6+ times). Positioning collapses in chaotic finales, especially when navigating back from clothing changes or through traffic. Dropped on even moderate climbs with 70km+ remaining.
key_patterns: Initiates sprint 250-300m out but lacks sustained power to line, repeatedly caught final 50m
Strong intermediate sprint performances don't translate to stage wins, suggesting positioning over pure speed deficit
Track omnium background provides technical cornering advantage but composure fails under contact/chaos
Two-kick sprint capability effective in uphill/headwind finishes when others fade early (2018 Tour)
Positioning deteriorated 2019-2022: following wrong wheels, wrong side barriers, misjudging re-entries post-crash
Team leadout quality declined post-2018; increasingly parasitic on rival trains with mixed success
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 observations show continued timing errors and crash vulnerability. Peak performance 2017 (4 Giro wins) declined through positioning failures 2019-2022, no wins observed recent seasons despite occasional podiums.
